event: admitted with body mass index of 67 kg/m2
underwent Da Vinci-assisted, laparoscopic, one-stage, single-anastomosis DS procedure
two-layer hand sewn anastomosis between the duodenum and the ileum was created
anastomosis was tested with methylene blue and air
drain was placed in left upper quadrant
upper gastrointestinal study (UGI) on postoperative day (POD) 2 was negative for a leak
computed tomography (CT) of the abdomen showing free fluid
taken to the operating room (OR) for diagnostic laparoscopy
active bleeding source was not identifiable
complete inspection of the gastroileostomy anastomosis was performed
no leakage seen with a methylene blue test
attempt to explore the duodenal stump was made
retraction of the duodenal stump and inflammation around the tissue
three large 19F Blake drains were placed
transfused with 2 units of packed red blood cells (PRBCs) intra-operatively
sent to intensive care unit (ICU) for further monitoring
abdominal drain had increased sanguinous output
taken back to the OR for exploratory laparotomy
large amount of hemoperitoneum was encountered
identified as coming from the gastroduodenal artery
duodenal stump was found to have total stump blow
Malecot tube was placed in the duodenum
received 5 units of PRBCs and 4 units of fresh frozen plasma intra-operatively
post-operative course was complicated by hemorrhagic fluid collections
post-operative course was complicated by atelectasis
developed acute thrombosis of the left internal jugular and subclavian veins
intravenous piperacillin/taxobactam was changed to cefazolin
home health care for dressing changes
